meaning: adj.装作聪明的,故作时髦的n.虚伪,小题大作
examples: Some of the shops were just a bit chichi, I must admit.
Two years ago glass bricks were all the rage in chichi homes.
ChiChi ran back and forth in front of his chair, straining at his leash as if to run down the beach.
"They rented a chichi apartment and spent a ton of money, " he recalls.
The chichi black shopping bags with swanky gold lettering and logos that everything gets delivered in are pretty cool, too.
